batch_summary.note: S-007: dance lesson lifestyle leverage Aug 2025 (ROW-14263/14276). 'Can't afford tap' deployed to Olivia. RF-06+RF-10+RF-03 compound. ARC-RF06-001 Signal 7.

messages.text: She said you haven't talked to her about it only that you couldn't afford tap
You're behind on daycare. You owed $360 due by August 28

primary_flags.classification: RF-06 primary Sev-1 — ROW-14263: Melissa tells Olivia that Eli 'couldn't afford tap' dance — using Eli's financial objection to the dance lesson as a negative characterization to Olivia. This is ADD-006 (lifestyle differential weaponized to child) + ADD-007 (dance lesson forced payment — Melissa enrolled despite Eli's SPP-backed objection at ROW-8107). Eli cited the SPP activity cost provision; Melissa enrolled anyway and then framed Eli's refusal to pay as 'can't afford it.' ROW-14276: concurrent daycare arrears pressure. ARC-RF06-001 Signal 7. Cross-ref RF-10 (unilateral enrollment) and RF-03 (Thursday schedule conflict from dance).
primary_flags.addendum_applied: ADD-006 (lifestyle differential — 'can't afford tap' deployed to Olivia). ADD-007 (dance lesson forced payment — enrolled despite SPP objection).
